assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the mythical role of the goddess *Nike*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The goddess Nike was a messenger goddess who brought word of victory.  

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/nike--winged-victory--of-samothrace--c--190-b-c-e)**

choices.content: She was a messenger goddess, who spread news of victory.
She was a protective goddess, who led troops into battle.
She was a sea goddess, who guided lost vessels to land.
She was a nature goddess, who caused the winds to blow.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Where would this sculpture originally have been seen?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cf5736f3bce6f368d3bbbc0445aefa14.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Originally located on the island of Samothrace, the *Winged Nike* stood on a large stone ship as part of an outdoor temple complex.   

 **[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/nike--winged-victory--of-samothrace--c--190-b-c-e)**

choices.content: As part of a temple complex at Samothrace
On a stone ship in an outdoor environment
On the pediment of a Doric temple at Samothrace
As part of the great Pergamon altar
On top of the tomb of a wounded soldier

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does the *Nike of Samothrace* appear to respond to its surroundings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The *Nike of Samothrace* was originally displayed outside standing on the bow of a stone ship.

Originally part of an outdoor temple complex near the sea, the sculpture's clinging drapery and fluttering wings appear to respond to the strong winds and sea mist. 

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/nike--winged-victory--of-samothrace--c--190-b-c-e)**

choices.content: She appears to be blown by the wind.
She appears to be humbled by nature.
She appears to glow in the sunlight.
She appears pulled down by gravity.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare these images of the goddess *Nike*.   Which feature distinguishes the *Nike of Samothrace* as a work from the Hellenistic period of Greek art? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/938f9e5253a8fc06dae35ed5e1718c5e.jpg) *Nike of Samothrace*


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/adc5191dfce22f2cfbb0054eeac02aa9.jpg) *Nike Adjusting Her Sandal*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The figure's wings have broken off the relief sculpture of *Nike Adjusting Her Sandal*.

Like Classical sculptors, Hellenistic artists created marble sculptures to reveal the anatomy of the human body.   Their work is distinguished by an emphasis on motion and emotion that heightens its dramatic effect.  

The *Nike of Samothrace* demonstrates this characteristic as the figure appears to move forward into the wind that causes the drapery to swirl around her and pushes back her wings.  

 **[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/nike--winged-victory--of-samothrace--c--190-b-c-e)**

choices.content: the clinging drapery that reveals the body
the figure is shown with wings
the use of marble as a medium

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare the *Nike of Samothrace* to these sculptures from the East Pediment of the Parthenon.  Match the visual characteristics with these Hellenistic and Classical sculptures?**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7f006d0ffc272ba50124f72eb1a4d823.jpg)

Sculpture from the East Pediment of the Parthenon (Classical)


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/938f9e5253a8fc06dae35ed5e1718c5e.jpg) 

*Nike of Samothrace* (Hellenistic) 
[[☃ categorizer 1]]

[[☃ categorizer 2]]
hints:
The varied surface textures in the *Nike of Samothrace* function to contrast the figure's fluttering wings to the transparent drapery that swirls around to reveal her body.  These details reinforce the energetic motion of the figure and create a dramatic effect typical of Hellenistic sculpture.

 **[Watch this video to review characteristics of Hellenistic art.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/nike--winged-victory--of-samothrace--c--190-b-c-e)**

In contrast to the dynamic energy of the Hellenistic period,  these Classical figures from the Parthenon exhibit emotional restraint in their harmonious balance and the calm relaxation of their graceful poses. Instead of the transparent drapery used in later periods, the women here appear in drapery that falls in linear patterns to reveal contours of the body beneath.  

 **[Watch this video to review characteristics of Classical art.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/phidias------sculpture-from-the-east-pediment-of-the-parthenon--c--448-432-b-c-e)**

items: a heightened feeling of drama and emotion
an interest in naturalism and anatomical forms
a use of drapery to reveal the female body
